question_text: A physics student compares two scenarios: Scenario 1 has a resultant force of 0 N on a moving object; Scenario 2 has a resultant force of 20 N on a stationary object. The student claims both objects are in equilibrium. Is this correct? Explain your reasoning using Newton's laws.
correct_answer: Only Scenario 1 is in equilibrium. Equilibrium requires resultant force = 0 N. Scenario 1 satisfies this (object moves at constant velocity); Scenario 2 does not (20 N resultant will cause acceleration). By Newton's first law, Scenario 2 is unbalanced.
explanation: Equilibrium is defined by a zero resultant force, which can occur whether an object is moving or stationary. Scenario 1 is correct: zero resultant means the object maintains constant velocity (or stays at rest). Scenario 2 is incorrect: a 20 N resultant force is unbalanced and will cause acceleration according to Newton's second law (F = ma).
